description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface, creating a striped pattern. The grooves are deep and regular, giving the block a ribbed appearance from above. The sides of the block are smooth and solid, with a matte black finish, contrasting with the lighter, marbled stone texture visible in the grooves. The block is thick and robust, suggesting it is designed to serve as a heavy-duty barrier or obstacle in an urban environment. The overall construction appears solid, with no visible joints or separations, indicating it is likely a single piece of carved or cast stone. There are no visible markings, engravings, or signs of significant weathering on the surfaces.
description: A thick, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface and smooth, solid black sides. The top features a marbled stone texture within the grooves, creating a striking striped pattern.
description_view_0: Front view showing the striped, grooved top surface and the solid black front face.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the marbled stone texture more visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the striped grooves and solid back face.
description_view_3: Opposite angled view, again emphasizing the deep grooves and the contrast between the marbled and black surfaces.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or on a prepared foundation
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering or erosion; surfaces appear clean and well-maintained.
